### 3.3.5 Alternative Recombinant Antibody Production

To overcome hybridoma limitations (expensive, slow, murine-derived):
- **Phage Display Libraries:** Antibody fragments (scFv, Fab) displayed on M13 phage coat protein pIII; selected by biopanning against antigen.
- **Transgenic Animals:** Knockout mice (e.g., XenoMouse, HuMAb-Mouse) carry human Ig loci; immunization produces fully human antibodies.
- **Single B-Cell Cloning:** Direct isolation of antigen-specific B-cells from patient blood, followed by RT-PCR of heavy/light chain genes.

### 3.3.6 Production in CHO Cells — Modern Industrial Scale

Recombinant mAbs are produced in **Chinese Hamster Ovary (CHO) cells** using:

$$
\text{Vector} \rightarrow \text{DHFR or GS selection system} \rightarrow \text{Gene amplification (methotrexate)} \rightarrow \text{Stable cell line} \rightarrow \text{Bioreactor}
$$

- **Fed-batch culture:** 10–14 days, 35 °C, pH 7.0, DO 40 %
- **Volumetric productivity:** **3–10 g·L⁻¹** in optimized platforms
- **Purification:** Protein A affinity chromatography → IEX → virus inactivation → UF/DF (Ultrafiltration/Diafiltration)

## 3.4 Comprehensive Comparison Matrix

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **KTU Board-Relevant Summary of Three Production Systems**

| Parameter | Insulin (Humulin) | Growth Hormone (Protropin) | Monoclonal Antibodies |
|---|---|---|---|
| Gene Source | Pancreatic β-cells mRNA | Pituitary mRNA / Synthetic | Hybridoma / Recombinant CHO |
| Vector | pBR322 | pBR322 / pET-series | pCHO or vectors with DHFR/GS |
| Host | *E. coli* K-12 | *E. coli* | CHO cells / Hybridoma |
| Promoter | *trp*, *lac*, *tac* | *trp*, *tac* | CMV, EF-1α |
| Protein Size | 5.8 kDa | 22 kDa | ~150 kDa |
| Disulfide Bonds | 3 | 2 | Multiple (heavy & light) |
| Glycosylation | None | None | N-linked at Asn297 |
| Inclusion Bodies | Yes (denatured + refolded) | Yes (refolded) | Secreted |
| Downstream Process | CNBr cleavage + RP-HPLC | Refolding + 4-step chromatography | Protein A + IEX + UF/DF |
| Year of Approval | 1982 | 1985 | 1986 (OKT3) / Modern mAbs ongoing |
| Yield Range | 50–100 mg·L⁻¹ | 1–5 g·L⁻¹ | 3–10 g·L⁻¹ |

# 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Question 1 (CO4 — L1: Remember)
> **[KTU University Exam — May 2024, Model Paper]**  
> Define the term **"recombinant protein"**. Mention any two expression hosts used for its production.

**Model Answer:**

A recombinant protein is a polypeptide encoded by a cloned gene, expressed in a heterologous host using rDNA technology. The recombinant protein should ideally retain the biological activity of its native counterpart.

**Two expression hosts:**
1. *Escherichia coli* — bacterial, fast-growing, cost-effective; lacks PTMs.
2. *Saccharomyces cerevisiae* — eukaryotic, performs post-translational modifications (N-glycosylation); well-characterized.

### Question 2 (CO4 — L2: Understand)
> **[KTU University Exam — Dec 2023]**  
> What is **Hybridoma technology**? Name the medium used for hybridoma selection and its key components.

**Model Answer:**

Hybridoma technology is the method developed by Köhler and Milstein (1975) for producing monoclonal antibodies by fusing B-lymphocytes (antibody producers) with myeloma cells (immortal partners) to create hybrid cells that combine the properties of both.

The selection medium is **HAT medium**, containing:
- **H**ypoxanthine
- **A**minopterin (blocks de novo nucleotide synthesis)
- **T**hymidine

Myeloma cells lacking **HGPRT** cannot use the salvage pathway and die in HAT, whereas unfused B-cells die naturally. Only hybridomas survive.

## 5.2 Part B Questions (14 Marks Each — KTU Internal Choice Format)

### Question A (14 Marks) — Choice 1

**[KTU University Exam — Dec 2024, Module 4, Q2A]**  
> **a)** Describe in detail the **production of recombinant human insulin** using *E. coli*, with a clear focus on the **separate-chain strategy** (7 marks).  
> **b)** Explain the **structural organization of proinsulin** and how it differs from mature insulin. Discuss the **enzymatic maturation process** (7 marks).

#### Model Answer:

**Part (a) — Separate-Chain Strategy (7 Marks)**

1. **mRNA Isolation and cDNA Synthesis (1 mark):** Pancreatic β-cell mRNA is reverse-transcribed into single-stranded cDNA, followed by Klenow fragment-driven second-strand synthesis.

2. **Cloning Vector Construction (1 mark):** ds-cDNA is ligated into pBR322 via homopolymeric dC/dG tailing at the PstI site. The recombinant plasmid is transformed into *E. coli* K-12.

3. **Separate Expression of A and B Chains (2 marks):**
   - The A-chain gene is fused downstream of the *lac* promoter with the *lacZ* (β-galactosidase) gene → IPTG-inducible A-chain fusion protein.
   - The B-chain gene is fused to the *trp* promoter with β-galactosidase → IAA-inducible B-chain fusion protein.
   - Two separate *E. coli* fermentations are run.

4. **Chemical Cleavage (1.5 marks):** Cyanogen bromide (CNBr) cleaves the fusion proteins at methionine residues, releasing authentic A and B chains with free N-termini.

5. **In Vitro Folding (1.5 marks):** A and B chains are combined in a redox buffer (oxidized/reduced glutathione, pH 8.5), allowing the formation of three correct disulfide bonds (two interchain, one intrachain A6-A11).

**Part (b) — Proinsulin Structure and Maturation (7 Marks)**

1. **Proinsulin Structure (2 marks):** Single polypeptide chain: **signal peptide (24 aa) — B-chain (30 aa) — C-peptide (31 aa) — A-chain (21 aa)**. The C-peptide connects B and A chains via dipeptide linkers (Arg-Arg and Lys-Arg).

2. **Differences from Mature Insulin (2 marks):**
   - Mature insulin is a 51 aa heterodimer (A + B chains), held by disulfide bonds.
   - Proinsulin is a single-chain precursor (86 aa) with C-peptide; has no biological insulin activity.

3. **Enzymatic Maturation (3 marks):**
   - **Signal peptide** is cleaved co-translationally in the ER by **signal peptidase**.
   - **Endopeptidases PC1/PC3** cleave at the **B-chain / C-peptide junction**.
   - **Endopeptidase PC2** cleaves at the **C-peptide / A-chain junction**.
   - **Carboxypeptidase E** removes the residual basic amino acids (Arg, Lys).
   - Mature insulin + C-peptide are stored in **secretory granules** and released in equimolar amounts upon glucose stimulation.

### Question B (14 Marks) — Choice 2

**[KTU University Exam — Dec 2024, Module 4, Q2B]**  
> **a)** Describe the **hybridoma technology** for the production of **monoclonal antibodies**. Explain the role of **HAT medium** and **limiting dilution** (7 marks).  
> **b)** Compare the four classes of therapeutic antibodies (murine, chimeric, humanized, fully human) with suitable examples (7 marks).

#### Model Answer:

**Part (a) — Hybridoma Technology (7 Marks)**

1. **Immunization (1 mark):** BALB/c mice are immunized with the target antigen + Freund's adjuvant. After 4–6 weeks, spleen contains antigen-specific B-cells.

2. **Fusion with PEG (1.5 marks):** Splenocytes are fused with HGPRT-deficient myeloma cells (e.g., SP2/0) using 50 % PEG 1450. PEG causes membrane destabilization, forming hybridomas.

3. **HAT Selection (2 marks):**
   - Aminopterin blocks de novo nucleotide synthesis.
   - Hypoxanthine + thymidine provide substrates for the **salvage pathway**, which requires **HGPRT**.
   - Myeloma cells (HGPRT-negative) cannot use salvage → die.
   - B-cells die naturally (apoptosis in culture).
   - Only hybridomas survive — they combine B-cell HGPRT with myeloma immortality.

4. **Screening (1 mark):** ELISA identifies hybridomas secreting antigen-specific antibodies.

5. **Limiting Dilution Cloning (1.5 marks):** Cells are diluted to 0.7 cells/well, ensuring each well contains a single progenitor → monoclonal hybridoma line.

**Part (b) — Therapeutic Antibody Classes (7 Marks)**

| Class | Composition | Human Content | Example (Target) | Year |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Murine | Entire antibody from mouse | 0 % | Muromonab-CD3 (CD3) | 1986 |
| Chimeric | Mouse variable + Human constant | ~ 65 % | Rituximab (CD20), Infliximab (TNF-α) | 1997 |
| Humanized | Mouse CDRs grafted onto human Ig | ~ 95 % | Trastuzumab (HER2), Bevacizumab (VEGF) | 1998 |
| Fully Human | Transgenic mice / phage display | 100 % | Adalimumab (TNF-α), Nivolumab (PD-1) | 2002 |

**Naming Convention (KTU favorite):** Suffix -omab (murine), -ximab (chimeric), -zumab (humanized), -umab (fully human).

## 5.3 KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Pitfall Callout

> [!WARNING]
> **Common Mistakes That Cost Marks in KTU Board Exams**
> 
> 1. **Confusing HAT mechanism:** Students often write "HAT kills myeloma" without explaining that HGPRT-deficient myeloma cells die because the **salvage pathway** (hypoxanthine + thymidine) cannot compensate when **aminopterin blocks de novo synthesis**. Write this explicitly.
> 
> 2. **Missing CNBr specificity:** Always state that cyanogen bromide cleaves at **methionine residues**, not just any amino acid.
> 
> 3. **Forgetting disulfide bonds:** Insulin has **3 disulfide bonds (2 interchain, 1 intrachain)** — omitting this loses 1 mark easily.
> 
> 4. **No mention of selection markers:** Always specify the **antibiotic resistance gene** (ampR, kanR, neoR) or **DHFR/GS** selection system when describing vectors.
> 
> 5. **Skipping affinity tags:** His₆ tag (Ni-NTA purification), Protein A (for mAbs) — worth mentioning for full marks.
> 
> 6. **Murine vs Chimeric confusion:** Suffixes -omab, -ximab, -zumab, -umab must be matched correctly with antibody class.
> 
> 7. **No yield/productivity numbers:** Examiners award marks for quantifying production (e.g., **CHO yields 3–10 g·L⁻¹**).

## 5.4 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember (Rapid Revision Checklist)

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **High-Yield Recall Points for KTU Board 2024 Scheme**

- [ ] **Humulin (1982, Genentech)** = First recombinant DNA therapeutic; insulin production used **separate-chain strategy** in *E. coli*.
- [ ] **Three disulfide bonds** in insulin: 2 interchain, 1 intrachain (A6-A11).
- [ ] **CNBr (cyanogen bromide)** cleaves fusion proteins at **methionine**.
- [ ] **Proinsulin maturation:** Trypsin + Carboxypeptidase B cleave **C-peptide**.
- [ ] **rhGH (Protropin, 1985):** 191 aa, 22 kDa, **2 disulfide bonds**, expressed in *E. coli* with **tac promoter**.
- [ ] **N-terminal methionine** of rhGH must be cleaved to match native sequence.
- [ ] **Hybridoma technology:** Köhler & Milstein, **1975**, Nobel 1984.
- [ ] **HAT medium** → HGPRT⁻ myeloma dies; only hybridomas survive.
- [ ] **Limiting dilution (0.7 cells/well)** ensures monoclonality.
- [ ] **Antibody classes:** -omab (murine), -ximab (chimeric), -zumab (humanized), -umab (fully human).
- [ ] **Modern mAb production** uses **CHO cells** with **DHFR/GS selection**, **fed-batch fermentation**, and **Protein A chromatography**.
- [ ] **Therapeutic antibody targets:** CD20 (Rituximab), HER2 (Trastuzumab), TNF-α (Adalimumab), PD-1 (Nivolumab).
- [ ] **Yields:** E. coli (10–30 % total protein), Pichia (10 g·L⁻¹), CHO (3–10 g·L⁻¹).
- [ ] **Molecular weight checkpoints:** Insulin ≈ 5.8 kDa, hGH ≈ 22 kDa, mAb ≈ 150 kDa.
- [ ] **PTMs:** Insulin and hGH — no glycosylation; mAb — N-glycosylation at Asn297.
- [ ] **Vector elements:** Promoter + RBS + MCS + Selection marker + Affinity tag.
